steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content:
The notes tell us that the traditional method “must mix hazardous chemicals with water” to dissolve indigo, whereas Rai’s process “discovered a process for dyeing blue jeans without chemicals.”
This opposition — hazardous chemicals vs. no chemicals — is the clearest signal that the two approaches differ.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the contrast
steps.content:
Choice D says Rai’s approach substitutes a natural plant‑based substance for the hazardous chemicals that manufacturers have traditionally used, directly echoing the contrast between hazardous chemicals and a chemical‑free method.
The other choices either describe only one approach, note a similarity, or state a fact without comparing the two methods.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
